metaDescriptionTemplate: Interactive album-book about the {{surname}} family genealogy
treeHelpIntro: This app helps you build a family tree and store texts and photos linked to relatives. You enter data in the admin; you view the tree and materials in the book — one place for your genealogy and stories.
treeHelpStep1: Add persons in the admin (Persons tab): name, surname, dates, parents.
treeHelpStep2: Add photos and texts (Photos and Texts tabs): upload files, create entries.
treeHelpStep3: Link them: on photos — who is in the picture; in texts — who is mentioned.
treeHelpStep4: Materials show in the person panel on the Tree tab: click a node to open a card with photos and texts.
treeHelpStep5: Set the root person (the tree is built from them) in the admin — the “root” column in the persons table.
treeHelpStep6: Editing is only in the admin; the book is view-only.
treeHelpStep7: Photo category (personal, group, other) affects how they are used for avatars.
treeHelpStep8: You can download the finished book as PDF — button in the left panel (available in both book and admin).
treeHelpData: Edited JSON must be replaced in the project manually; copy photos into folders yourself.
treeHelpPerSectionBefore: On each tab and in the admin there is
treeHelpPerSectionBold: Help — check tips for the current section.
personSummary_datesPartial_2: Only part of the timeline is known: {{dates}}.
personSummary_datesUnknown_1: Exact birth and death dates are unknown.
personSummary_datesUnknown_2: Reliable life dates are not available.
personSummary_datesUnknown_3: No exact life dates have been preserved.
personSummary_workAndCities_1: Worked as {{occupation}}; cities where {{subjectPronoun}} lived or stayed: {{cities}}.
personSummary_workAndCities_2: Occupation: {{occupation}}. Cities mentioned in life records: {{cities}}.
personSummary_workAndCities_3: {{subjectPronoun}} lived or stayed in several cities: {{cities}}. Occupation: {{occupation}}.
personSummary_cityOnlyMany_2: Records mention multiple cities of residence or visits: {{cities}}.
personSummary_cityOnlyMany_3: {{subjectPronoun}} lived or stayed in several cities: {{cities}}.
personSummary_comment_2: A note in the record says: {{comment}}.
treeResetRootAlready: The tree root already matches the default value from JSON
historySelectHint: Select a text from the list
familySearchShowParents: Also show parent-only rows (no own principal birth/death)
familySearchSearchPending: Typing… results update in a moment
familySearchSortColumnHint: Sort by this column; click again to reverse order
mapArchiveSearchAria: Search the map archive by person name
mapArchiveYearRangeDragAria: Drag to move the selected year span
mapLayerPrizyv: Draft office totals (Memory of the People)
mapPodvigExternalSiteHint: Links open the official Russian Ministry of Defence “Podvig Naroda” site. A “connection not secure” or certificate warning usually comes from your network, proxy, or security software—not from this book. Try another connection (e.g. mobile data) or open the link later.
mapPrizyvAttribution: Aggregated enlistment-office totals; source «Memory of the People» (Pamjat naroda).
bookHelpMapUnifiedIntro: Use the list under the map title to switch data: family places, indexed FamilySearch events, or conscription-office distribution.
bookHelpMapPrizyv1: The third option shows how many people from the family dataset are linked to each military enlistment office in the archival data (no year timeline — totals only).
bookHelpMap2Tab1: This map shows indexed historical events (birth, death, etc.) from a FamilySearch export, placed at preset coordinates for each location.
bookHelpMap2Tab2: Drag the year range on the slider to filter events; the number above the bar shows how many events match.
bookHelpMap2Tab3: Click a marker for details and a link to the record on FamilySearch. Coordinates are fixed per location name in the project data.
bookHelpPersons1: In the “Persons” tab, use the search field to quickly find someone by surname or name.
bookHelpPersons2: Click a person card to open their page with key facts, relations, photos, and related stories.
bookHelpPersons3: The “Go to person” button opens the same person in the tree for relation navigation.
bookHelpHistory1: In the “Texts” tab, select an item from the list on the left; the full text appears on the right.
bookHelpHistory2: Use search to quickly find stories by title and content.
bookHelpHistory3: From a person card, use “Mentioned in stories” to open related texts.
bookHelpPhotos1: In the “Photos” tab, open a photo by clicking its thumbnail.
bookHelpPhotos2: In photo view, switch front/back with “Show back or front of photo”.
bookHelpPhotos3: Use “Open full size” to view the photo in fullscreen.
bookHelpPhotos4: “Show labels / Hide labels” toggles names of people marked on the photo.
bookHelpMap1: The map shows relatives’ birth places and cities of residence from each person’s data on an interactive OpenStreetMap view.
bookHelpMap2: In the “Family tree data” mode, use the dropdown in the top-right corner to show all people on the map or focus on one selected person.
bookHelpMap3: Use the reset button to return to all markers; you can also zoom with the mouse wheel or +/− controls and drag the map with the left mouse button.
bookHelpMap4: The map is built from saved city coordinates (placeFallbacks). If a city has no coordinates yet, it will not appear on the map.
bookHelpMap5: Add new cities and coordinates in Admin: when a city field loses focus, you can save detected coordinates or set the point manually on a mini-map.
photoToggleBack: Show back or front of photo
adminRootChangeConfirm: Set this person as root (tree and book title will be based on them)?
adminDeleteRowsConfirm: Do you want to delete {{count}} rows?
adminDownloaded: JSON downloaded to your Downloads folder
helpPersonsAdd: Click «Add row», then fill in the person's data (name, surname, dates, etc.).
helpPersonsRoot: Root is the person the tree is built from (marked ★). To change root, click «—» in that person's row and confirm.
helpPersonsDelete: To delete a person, click the ✕ button at the beginning of the row.
helpPersonsCitiesCoords: For “Place of birth” and “City of residence”, city validation runs on blur. If coordinates are found, you can save them; if not, enter lat/lon manually or pick a point on the map in the dialog.
adminPhotosHowItWorks: Create a public/photos/ folder and upload photos — they will appear here after you refresh the list.
helpPhotosFolders: Folder structure can be anything, but it's better to keep it simple.
helpPhotosNewBadge: New photos without data will be marked with a «new» badge.
helpPhotosSteps: Fill in the photo caption, choose its category — personal, group or other — and mark persons on it. Then these photos will be linked to the selected persons.
adminPhotosNoPhotos: No photos. Add files to public/photos/ and click «Refresh list».
adminPhotoDrawHint: Pick shape, click on photo. Circle — drag from center.
adminPhotoFaceRectHint: Mark one corner, then the diagonally opposite corner (any direction). Or drag to draw.
adminSelectEntry: Select a text from the list
adminShowAllConfirm: Make all photos visible in the book?
adminHideAllConfirm: Hide all photos from the book?
adminDeleteAllConfirm: Delete all photos from the list?
adminHistoryHowItWorks: Left: list of texts. Right: editor. Edit title and text, link persons to the text.
adminSaveReminder: After editing, copy data to clipboard or download JSON (buttons on the left).
kinDescGrandchild: child of a son or daughter
personSummary_birthYearFragment: born in {{birth}} (exact birth date not preserved)
personSummary_deathYearFragment: passed away in {{death}} (exact death date not preserved)
personBioSpouseUnknownAlive: There is no spouse information yet.
personBioSpouseUnknownDeceased: Spouse information has not been preserved.
personBioParents_3: In the parental line, {{relatives}} are mentioned.
personSummary_cityOnly_3: According to available data, lived in {{city}}.
